type.fr: Commentaires généraux à propos de l'installation

comments.comment: This facility is comprised of a military garrison as well as a range and training area. The garrison is a built up area and the infrastructure houses various military units and support agencies. The range and training area is approximately 22,000 hectares and is used for military training activities including live fire training and dry training. The range and training area contains infrastructure to support various types of military training such as small arms ranges, a grenade range, mechanical target ranges, impact areas for artillery, a maneuver corridor, an urban operations site, live fire shoot houses, and a rappel site and rappel tower. The range and training area is used year round by military units stationed at the facility as well as visiting units. On site releases in the range and training area fluctuate throughout the year depending on the frequency and intensity of the military training occurring.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: The amount of copper sent for recycling in 2013 was the result of a brass sale from copper ammunition cartridges collected from military training.  The previous year, the amount of copper sent for recycling was due to a firing range being remediated and the copper content of the ammunition taken out of the range being sent for recycling.  This is why there is a difference in the amounts of copper sent for recycling from 2012 to 2013.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: None of the firing ranges were remediated for spent ammunition in 2013, so there was no lead by-products sent for recycling.  In 2012, there was a firing range that was remediated and lead by-product sent for recycling, which is why there is a change in quantities of lead recycled from 2012 to 2013.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: The amount of zinc sent for recycling in 2013 was the result of a brass sale from ammunition cartridges collected from military training.  The previous year, the amount of zinc sent for recycling was due to a firing range being remediated and the zinc content of the ammunition taken out of the range being sent for recycling.  This is why there is a difference in the amounts of zinc sent for recycling from 2012 to 2013.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Difference in releases of nitrogen oxides from the previous year is due to increased natural gas usage at the facility central heating plant as it was not in full operation in 2018, but came back into full operation in early 2019.  In addition, fuel usage for diesel generators, propane and fuel oil for heating fluctuates from year to year.

comments.comment: There were no disposals of copper in 2019 as there were no remediation projects of the small arms range backstops that contain copper from ammunition fired.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment:
This substance is only under the threshold by less than 0.1 tonne; therefore, is being voluntarily reported.
Releases of nitrogen oxides at this facility are due to natural gas, fuel oil, and propane for heating, diesel and propane use for emergency generating power, and releases to air from firing/detonating of certain munitions during military training.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Release of nitrous oxides is slightly lower than the previous reporting year due to lower natural gas usage, lower emergency generator usage, and lower amounts of releases to air from firing ammunition/ordnance destruction.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: Releases of carbon monoxide at this facility are due to natural gas, fuel oil, and propane for heating, diesel and propane use for emergency generating power, and releases to air from firing/detonating of certain munitions during military training.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Releases of carbon monoxide are slightly lower than the previous reporting year due to lower natural gas usage, lower emergency generator usage, and lower amounts of releases to air from firing ammunition/ordnance destruction.

type.fr: Nature des autres rejets au sol

comments.comment: Met the threshold for off-site disposal/recycling, but not for on-site releases.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Quantities of copper reported for released to air and land are lower than the previous reporting year, due to decreased levels of military training during the first few months of the COVID-19 pandemic.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Three of the small arms range backstops was due for remediation/repair in 2020. Soil containing
copper fragments from fired ammunition was removed from the backstop and transferred to
the receiver for processing/disposal.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: The quantity of recycled copper was due to the trap remediation of an indoor firing range with salvaged ammunition from the bullet trap being sent for recycling.  There was no sale of brass casings in 2020 for the facility, which is why the amount sent for recycling is lower than the previous reporting year.

type.en: Nature of other releases to land
type.fr: Nature des autres rejets au sol

comments.comment: Amount of lead released to land is due to military training activities; specifically firing of ammunition containing lead that is caught in an earth back stop.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: Quantities of lead that are released to land and air are mainly due to military training involving firing of ammunition.  Quantities of lead that is disposed of off-site is due to remediation of earth back stops that have had ammunition fired into it.  Quantities of lead sent for recycling is due to salvaged fired ammunition remediation for bullet traps in an indoor range.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Quantities of lead reported for released to air and land are lower than the previous reporting year, due to decreased levels of military training during the first few months of the COVID-19 pandemic.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Three of the small arms range backstops were due for remediation/repair in 2020. Soil containing
lead fragments from fired ammunition was removed from the backstop and transferred to
the receiver for processing/disposal.  In addition, soil from a decommissioned range that already had bullets screened from it previously, had to sent for disposal.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: The quantity of recycled lead was due to the trap remediation of an indoor firing range with salvaged ammunition from the bullet trap being sent for recycling.  The amount sent for recycling is higher than the previous reporting year as there were no bullet trap cleanings that year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: The reporting threshold was not met for this substance, but it is part of a product that was released on-site and sent for disposal/recycling that also contains lead and copper.  This is ammunition that is fired on site, as well as ammunition remediated from back stops of firing ranges and sent for disposal/recycling.

type.en: Nature of other releases to land
type.fr: Nature des autres rejets au sol

comments.comment: Amount of zinc released to land is due to military training activities; specifically firing of ammunition containing zinc that is caught in an earth back stop.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Quantities of zinc reported for released to land are lower than the previous reporting year, due to decreased levels of military training during the first few months of the COVID-19 pandemic.  Quantities of zinc released to air are slightly higher the previous reporting year due to increase use of No2 fuel oil for heating.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Three of the small arms range backstops were due for remediation/repair in 2020. Soil containing
zinc fragments from fired ammunition was removed from the backstop and transferred to
the receiver for processing/disposal.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: The quantity of recycled zinc was due to the trap remediation of an indoor firing range with salvaged ammunition from the bullet trap being sent for recycling.  There was no sale of brass casings in 2020 for the facility, which is why the amount sent for recycling is lower than the previous reporting year.

comments.comment: Amount of copper released to land is due to military training activities; specifically firing of ammunition containing copper that is caught in an earth back stop.  Copper sent for disposal is from remediation of soil in the earth back stop and then its disposal at a receiving facility.  Copper sent for recycling is from metal ammunition casings that are collected and sold to a recycling facility.

comments.comment:
Two small arms range backstops underwent remediation/repair in 2021. Soil containing
copper fragments from fired ammunition was removed from the backstop and transferred to
the receiver for processing/disposal.  The amount of copper sent for disposal is less than the previous year as there were three ranges remediated in 2020.
